title: Your Partner's Money Habits Will Cost You $760K
hook: If you and your partner don't agree on money, you're not just arguing — you're statistically losing three-quarters of a million dollars.
angle: Personal finance never talks about the relational dimension — but financial misalignment in relationships is one of the single biggest destroyers of long-term wealth, backed by Kansas State University research.
audience: Couples in their 20s-40s, people who argue about money with a partner, anyone considering marriage or cohabitation
key_numbers: 70% of couples argue about money regularly
37% more likely to divorce if money fights are frequent (Kansas State University)
Average divorce costs $15,000–$30,000 in legal fees alone
Financially misaligned couples retire with $760,000 less than aligned ones
